summary: Full backend + frontend regression of the Bark&Bond pay-on-outcome match engine (iter 2 redesign). 32/32 backend pytest cases pass and every UI flow listed in the request works end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
action_items: (none) — system behaves to spec. Optional: in /api/intros, billing_status is hard-coded 'billed' — when Stripe gets wired in, capture the real PaymentIntent id rather than the literal string.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~580 lines; still single-file but well-sectioned. Fine for now.
/api/conversions does not pass `request` so client IP is not captured for fraud scoring (intros do). Consider parity.

engine.recompute_pricing uses median over only suburbs that produced intros in the last 7d; an empty intro pool yields median=1 → all suburbs land on 0.6× (3.00 fee). That is the cold-start state observed in oversight (Eastern Suburbs and Ormond at 0.60× / A$3.00). Acceptable but worth documenting so it isn't read as a bug.

Submission auto-publish path inserts a trainer document with verification_status='hold' when 0.6 ≤ conf < 0.85 (status='auto_published_unverified'). The subsequent verification_loop will re-score it; that's the intended self-healing behaviour.
Ops UI surfaces an 'Invalid passcode' error toast on wrong pass — confirmed working. Good UX.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (rewritten for iter 2 — match engine + intros + conversions + submissions + oversight + legacy-removed assertions, 32 tests)
seed_data_creation: DB was already auto-seeded at startup with the 12 real Melbourne trainers from services/seed.py. Tests created realistic submissions (auto-published 'Positive K9 Training Melbourne', auto-held 'Weak Submission Test' from UI plus a few from API tests) and a handful of intros + conversions that show up in oversight (revenue A$276.00, 4 intros / 4 conversions / 24h). No test-only deletion endpoint exists by design (no admin mutations); leftover test rows are by design and harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the iter-2 regression baseline. AI calls (match, score, SEO) take 5-30s — keep timeouts ≥60s. The TestSubmissions::test_strong_evidence_auto_publishes test is sensitive to AI judgement: it now uses the *real* Positive K9 Training business with consistent domain + bio so Claude scores ≥0.6. Do not introduce 'TEST_' prefixes or random suffixes into business names — Claude will (correctly) flag them as fabricated and the test will land in 'held'. The TestLegacyRemoved class enforces that none of the iter-1 endpoints (admin/*, /trainers list, /featured, /leads, /suburbs, /categories, /stats/public) come back; flip those if the design ever brings them back. data-testids confirmed: match-input/match-submit/match-results-list, result-<id>, result-connect-<id>, connect-{name,email,phone,desc,submit,success}, conversion-confirm, submit-{name,suburb,...,go,result}, ops-{pass-input,login-submit,refresh,signout,northstar}.
